label: Phase 0
title: Preparation
description: Evaluate technology transfer partner (TTP) proposals, conduct technical and IP due diligence, define the full IP scope across all 9 domains and 142 IP items, negotiate and sign the Technology Transfer Agreement (TTA), and prepare all enabling infrastructure. The IP definitions, warranty terms, license scope, royalty structure, and legal obligations established here govern the entire project lifetime. Errors or gaps at this stage propagate through every subsequent phase.
activities: Evaluate TTP proposals: assess technical capability, IP portfolio completeness, and delivery track record against all 142 IP items across 9 domains
Conduct IP due diligence: map each of the 71 T1 (Must Transfer) IPs to TTP's proposed deliverables; identify gaps and negotiate gap-closure obligations in the TTA

Negotiate Technology Transfer Agreement (TTA): define license grant (R0/R1/R1*/R2 per IMEC IAP model), IP warranty, royalty structure with cap, transfer session scope, and warranty on process sufficiency
Set up Document Management System (DMS): deploy before any classified documents are received; define taxonomy, access control, and audit trail (N7)
Recruit and mobilize engineering team: all 8 engineer groups (N1–N8) must be available before P1 begins — process engineers recruited and sent for pre-training if possible
Conduct site feasibility study: evaluate candidate locations for cleanroom suitability, utility access, environmental permitting, and Vietnamese regulatory compliance (N8)
Define equipment vendor evaluation criteria and issue RFQ to equipment vendors (N4)
Establish quality plan v1.0: define phase gate acceptance criteria, JEDEC qualification plan structure, and reliability test schedule aligned with P2/P3 milestones (N6)
Submit technology transfer registration dossier to Vietnam MOST within 90 days of TTA signing [VN-TTL Art.31; VN-D76 Arts.13–17]
deliverables: Signed Technology Transfer Agreement (TTA) — with IP scope Schedule A identifying all 142 IP items by category and delivery commitment
DMS operational with document taxonomy, RBAC access control, and audit trail — ready to receive classified technology documents
IP scope Schedule A: each of the 142 IPs mapped to TTP's delivery phase, completeness criteria, and accepted format
Engineering team mobilization report: all 8 groups staffed (process, PI, PDK, equipment, metrology, Q&R, IT, facility)
Site feasibility study report: candidate site selected with environmental permits approved
Quality Plan v1.0: phase gate criteria (P2 exit: yield ≥ 60%; P3 exit: yield ≥ 80%), JEDEC test schedule, Vietnamese MOST compliance plan
Equipment vendor preferred list: 2–3 qualified vendors per tool type with evaluation scores
Construction RFP issued and contractor selected
Vietnam MOST technology transfer registration dossier submitted [VN-TTL Art.31] — certificate target within 5 working days of complete dossier
refs: IMEC IAP Bilateral Contract Model — Leten, Vanhaverbeke et al. 2013 (open access, fully read)
Micron Technology Transfer License Agreement (TTLA) §2.2 License Grant; §3.2 Transfer Deliverables (read)
DSP §9.2 IP Sufficiency Warranty (read)
JEDEC JEP012A / JEP013A / JEP013B — Foundry Process Qualification Guidelines (image-PDF, accepted)
Vietnam Technology Transfer Law (TTL 07/2017/QH14) Art.23 Mandatory Contract Content; Art.31 Registration
Vietnam Government Decree 76/2018/NĐ-CP Arts.13–17 Registration Procedure
ISO 9001:2015 Quality Management Systems — Requirements (training-derived; not uploaded)
entry_criteria: Business decision to establish new fab confirmed; Technology Transfer Partner (TTP) shortlisted; budget approved; project steering committee formed.
exit_criteria: TTA signed and registered; IP scope (Schedule A) finalized and agreed; DMS operational and document taxonomy defined; Team fully recruited; construction and equipment procurement plans approved; Vietnamese MOST technology transfer registration dossier submitted.
image_caption: Schematic representation of IP model within an IIAP program

label: Phase 1-TT
title: Technology Transfer Stream
subtitle: Parallel Stream A
description: Receive all technology transfer deliverables from TTP. Audit each deliverable against the IP scope in Schedule A for completeness, accuracy, and usability. Co-develop additional IPs or test vehicles with TTP where gaps are identified or Green Fab's tool set differs from TTP's. Conduct structured on-site training at TTP's fab for all relevant engineering groups (process, integration, PDK, metrology, quality, IT/automation). This stream runs simultaneously with P1b (Equipment) and P1c (Construction).
activities: Receive and log all technology deliverables in DMS: classify each document by IP category, security level, and receiving engineer group
Audit process recipe deliverables (N1): verify completeness of each process module recipe — tool type compatibility, parameter values, process window limits, safety notes, and PM dependency
Audit integration flow documentation (N2): verify all process steps, Q-time specifications, cross-module interface rules, and contamination zone definitions are complete
Audit PDK deliverables (N3): run TTP's SPICE models through Cadence/HSPICE validation; run DRC on TTP's reference test chip layout (0 errors expected); verify standard cell library completeness
Audit reliability model deliverables (N6): verify TDDB, BTI, HCI, EM lifetime models include activation energies, acceleration factors, and model forms consistent with JEDEC JEP001 requirements
Audit metrology methodology and measurement definitions (N5): verify each measurement type has tool assignment, recipe structure, calibration reference, and acceptance limits
Audit IT/automation IP (N7): verify SECS/GEM DCV master list completeness, FDC rule framework structure, dispatch logic documentation

Conduct on-site training at TTP: process engineers shadow TTP process engineers for each module; PDK engineers review model calibration methodology; metrology engineers observe OPC calibration campaign
Co-develop test vehicle design with TTP integration team (N2, N3): define PCM test structures, integration evaluation structures, and OPC calibration targets for use in P2
Document all gaps: produce IP Completeness Audit Report and escalate any missing T1 IP items to TTP project manager with contractual remedy timeline
deliverables: IP Completeness Audit Report: all 142 IPs assessed — received/audited/gap status for each item; signed off by both Green Fab and TTP project managers
All 71 T1 (Must Transfer) IP deliverables received, logged in DMS, and audited as complete and usable
Training completion records
Test Vehicle Design Specification

Process Integration Set: node overview & migration strategy 28 nm→16 nm; Golden Process Flow (FEOL/MOL/BEOL) with thermal budgets, split points, decision trees, Q-time control; Process of Record (POR); process features & module options (SiGe S/D, LVT/RVT/HVT, eNVM/RF/HV, auto/industrial); BOM; FMEA; control plan; integration control documentation; implant condition; raw material (wafer substrate spec, qualified wafer vendor); TEM/XTEM atlases with metrology specs & acceptance windows; CD monitor pattern and spec list; CD-SEM OI (incl. CD-SEM recipe); thickness monitor pattern/spec list/measurement site map (incl. film stack); inspection layer; process qualification report; WLR qualification test key

Design Enablement Set: Design Rule Manual (DRM) and layer map; device & SRAM targets, param corners, characterization plans; short-circuit/IDDQ/IDDA/ROSC targets and screens; reliability plan, stress methods & results (HTOL/HAST/TC/THB/HTS/PCT; BTI/HCI/TDDB/EM)

Manufacturing Enablement Set: mask set definitions & RET/OPC/MDP guidance (photo alignment kit incl. alignment tree/EQ type/overlay spec, photo condition & EBR setting table, AG/OPC apply form and record, MDP, mask specification); BEOL stack options & thick-metal variants; process specifications (module-level specs, SPC/APC hooks, control limits); tool/material/special gas specifications with qualified vendors and alternates; evaluation methodology & scoring, extendibility to next node; yield ramp, process control, defect control playbooks; WAT monitor items and spec; WAT pin assignment; WAT Cpk review; in-line KIP/Non-KIP item summary; test-key and layout document
PDK and EDA Set: PDK content & staged releases (v0.1/v0.5/v0.9/v1.0), models, decks, views; IP strategy & portfolio (standard-cell, IOs, SRAM/ROM/eNVM, PHYs) and licensing model

Governance Set: document catalog & configuration control SOPs, export-control tags, audit trail; know-how transfer plan & training program; deliverables list, test vehicles, evaluation forms & score matrices; engineering change notice (ECN); material/equipment/process qualification plan; yield management procedure; security management procedure
refs: TTA Schedule A (IP Portfolio) — contractual instrument signed in P0
JEDEC JEP012A — Foundry Process Qualification, Front End Transistor Level (uploaded, image-PDF)
JEDEC JEP013B — Foundry Process Qualification: Technology Qualification Vehicle Testing (uploaded, image-PDF)
JEDEC JEP122H — Failure Mechanisms and Models for Semiconductor Devices (uploaded, text-verified — Table 6-1 model parameters)
JEDEC JEP132A — Process Characterization (uploaded, image-PDF)
IMEC p.56–57 — R0/R1/R1*/R2 IP categories and bilateral contracts
Micron TTLA §3.2 — Transfer Deliverables (process information, design information, wafers, sessions)
May & Spanos (2006) Ch.2–4 — Process Module Technology
BSIM Group UC Berkeley (2017) — BSIM4 Model Validation
entry_criteria: TTA signed; TTP's internal transfer team mobilized; Green Fab engineers recruited and available for training; DMS ready to receive classified documents.
exit_criteria: All T1 (Must Transfer) IP deliverables received and audited as complete; all T2 (Should Transfer) IPs received or gap-closure plan agreed; training sessions at TTP completed for all engineering groups; IP completeness audit report signed off by both parties.
image_caption: Ví dụ chuyển giao cụm fab (Infineon front-end DRAM fab cluster)

label: Phase 1-EQ
title: Equipment Stream
subtitle: Parallel Stream B
description: Issue Purchase Orders to equipment and material vendors, co-develop equipment process recipes and specifications to match TTP's transferred process requirements, conduct training at vendor sites, execute Factory Acceptance Tests (FAT) at each vendor, manage equipment delivery and cleanroom installation, hook up all utilities, establish SECS/GEM automation integration, and execute Site Acceptance Tests (SAT) to formally transfer tool ownership to Green Fab. This stream runs simultaneously with P1a (Technology Transfer) and P1c (Construction).
activities: Issue Purchase Orders to all equipment vendors: process tools (litho, etch, CVD, PVD, ALD, CMP, implant, RTA, clean), metrology tools (CD-SEM, overlay, film thickness, defect inspection), and facility equipment
Co-develop equipment process recipes with vendors aligned with TTP's transferred technology: verify vendor tools can replicate key process parameters from transferred recipes (N1, N4)

Conduct training at vendor sites: equipment engineers shadow vendor FSE for PM procedures, safety interlocks, and process-specific calibration (N4); IT engineers test SECS/GEM interface compliance at vendor site (N7)

Execute Factory Acceptance Tests (FAT) at each vendor site: process performance on vendor test wafers (N1); safety interlock verification (N4 lead); SECS/GEM E30/E37/E87/E90/E94 compliance testing (N7); metrology GR&R study (N5); vendor quality document review (N6)

Manage equipment delivery and cleanroom move-in: coordinate with N8 on construction timeline; verify cleanroom ISO class before tool move-in; execute utility hook-up checklist at each connection point
Execute utility hook-up: verify UPW, gas, chemical, power, exhaust connections at each tool position (N4, N8); commission SECS/GEM host connection and register each tool in MES (N7)

Execute Site Acceptance Tests (SAT): repeat FAT test protocol in Green Fab's utility environment; sign SAT certificate and formally transfer tool ownership; initiate warranty period and first PM cycle
Deploy baseline FDC rules for each tool from TTP's transferred FDC framework (N7, N4)
deliverables: FAT certificates for all critical-path process and metrology tools — signed by both Green Fab and vendor; punch-list items resolved or formally tracked
SAT certificates for all installed tools — formal tool ownership transferred to Green Fab; warranty period initiated
SECS/GEM connectivity verification report: each tool visible in MES with DCV data collection confirmed operational
PM programs established in CMMS for all tools: intervals, parts BOM, procedures documented
Baseline FDC rules deployed and operational: process data streaming from each tool to FDC server confirmed
Tool-to-tool matching baseline: same-type tools measured on common reference wafers; inter-tool offsets documented
Utility hook-up completion report: all connection points verified to specification (UPW resistivity, gas purity, chemical concentration, power quality) at each tool
refs: SEMI E10-1117 — Equipment Reliability, Availability, Maintainability
SEMI E30/E37/E87/E90/E94 — SECS/GEM Standards
SEMI E133-0307 — FDC Specification
sgsystemsglobal.com — FAT Vendor Qualification (2024)
Polar Semiconductor — Equipment Engineer responsibilities (2024)
AIAG APQP 2nd ed. — FAT/SAT methodology
entry_criteria: Equipment vendor list finalized; PO budget approved; key equipment specifications defined from TTP technology transfer; cleanroom construction at sufficient completion stage for delivery.
exit_criteria: All critical-path tools hooked up and SAT-accepted; SECS/GEM connections to MES verified; tool-to-tool matching within specification; FDC baseline deployed; all utility connections at tool points verified; tool qualification (P2 entry) ready to begin.

label: Phase 1-CB
title: Fab Construction Stream
subtitle: Parallel Stream C
description: Phase 1c — Construction & Building Stream runs in parallel with P1a/P1b and delivers the physical facility. Cleanroom construction targets the airborne-particle class required by the technology node (ISO Class 5/Class 100 for litho bay, ISO Class 6 for general fab, ISO Class 7 for support areas) [1]. Critical utilities are sized for full committed HVM capacity: ultra-pure-water (UPW) system with resistivity >18.2 MΩ·cm and TOC <1 ppb, bulk and specialty gas distribution, process chemical delivery, deionized-water cooling, vacuum and exhaust systems with abatement, electrical capacity with N+1 redundancy on critical buses, and emergency-power coverage for tools that cannot tolerate sudden shutdown. Vibration isolation, magnetic-field isolation, and seismic provisions follow the tool vendors' site-prep specifications. Construction is sequenced for ladder release to enable parallel equipment installation (P1b) as floor sections complete. Building handover requires a documented site-acceptance package and clean construction history (no organic outgassing residues).
activities: Develop cleanroom design specifications from process requirements: ISO class per zone (ISO 3 for litho bays, ISO 4–5 general fab), airflow volume, temperature/humidity tolerances, vibration isolation requirements — inputs from N1, N4, N5 based on TTP's transferred material selection table
Select and contract architect (cleanroom design specialist) and general contractor: issue detailed construction RFP with all utility specifications
Construct cleanroom shell: structural foundation, wall and ceiling systems, raised floor, sub-fab layout for utility distribution
Install HVAC system: Fan Filter Units (FFUs), Air Handling Units (AHUs), ductwork, make-up air units — achieve laminar airflow at design velocity
Commission UPW system: achieve resistivity ≥ 18.2 MΩ·cm, TOC < 1 ppb, bacteria < 0.001 CFU/mL, particles < 1/mL at 0.05 µm (N8)
Commission bulk gas systems (N2, O2, Ar, H2) and specialty gas systems (SiH4, NF3, Cl2, HBr, NH3): verify purity at point-of-use; install leak detection and purifiers (N8)
Commission chemical distribution systems: HF, H2O2, TMAH, NH4OH, IPA — test N2 blanket, leak detection, neutralization, waste treatment (N8)
Install and commission power distribution, UPS, and emergency backup systems
Commission abatement systems: verify DRE ≥ 99.999% for PFC gases (N8)
Deploy IT/OT network infrastructure: install OT switches, configure IT/OT firewall, deploy MES application servers, server room commissioning (N7)

Conduct Facility Readiness Test (FRT): ISO 14644-1 particle count certification at all zones; airflow volume and uniformity; temperature/humidity stability; all utility systems at design specification
deliverables: ISO 14644-1 cleanroom certification report: particle count results for each zone meeting target class (ISO 3 for litho bays; ISO 4–5 for general fab areas)
UPW system commissioning report: all quality parameters met (resistivity, TOC, bacteria, particle count) with continuous monitoring operational
Gas system commissioning report: purity verified at point-of-use for all bulk and specialty gases; leak detection operational
Chemical distribution commissioning report: concentration accuracy, N2 blanket integrity, leak detection, waste neutralization all verified
Abatement system certification: DRE measurement results for each abatement unit
Facility Readiness Test (FRT) certificate — signed by N8 (Facility), N4 (Equipment), N1 (Process), and N7 (IT) confirming facility ready to receive process tools
IT/OT network commissioning report: MES deployed, OT network operational, server room active — ready for SECS/GEM connections in P1b
Environmental permits: all construction-phase environmental compliance certificates obtained
refs: ISO 14644-1 Cleanroom Air Cleanliness Classes; ITRS / IRDS facility roadmaps (training-derived)
entry_criteria: Site selected; environmental permits obtained; architect and construction contractor engaged; fab design specifications defined from process and equipment requirements.
exit_criteria: Cleanroom certified to ISO 14644-1 target class (particle count, airflow); UPW, gas, chemical, power, HVAC, and abatement systems commissioned to specification; Facility Readiness Test (FRT) signed off; cleanroom ready to receive process tools.

label: Phase 2
title: Test Vehicle · PDK v0.9 · Yield ≥ 60%
description: Phase 2 — Test Vehicle and PDK v0.9 validates that the locally-built fab can actually execute the transferred technology. Test-vehicle wafer lots (≥3 consecutive qualification lots × 25 wafers) run end-to-end through the full process. Yield ramp targets functional yield ≥60% on test vehicle within the phase. The test structures include device DOE arrays, SRAM compilers, ring oscillators, and reliability test patterns per JEDEC Foundry Technology Qualification Vehicle Testing JEP013B [1] and the integrator-level Foundry Process Qualification JEP012A/JEP013A [2,3]. Reliability qualification stress chambers are loaded (HTOL/HAST/TC) and time-zero readings are taken; the qualification will complete in P3. Process FMEA per JEDEC JEP131A [4] is completed for all critical modules with mitigation plans for high-RPN failure modes. PDK is refined from v0.5 to v0.9 via DTCO loops — SPICE corner models recalibrated from local silicon, DRC/LVS/PEX rules updated from hotspot analysis, and standard-cell timing re-extracted. The yield-learning curve follows the canonical three-period model (introduction → rapid learning → plateau) described in May & Spanos Sec. 5.7 [5].
activities: Load transferred process recipes on all qualified tools; run tool-to-tool matching qualification wafers to verify all same-type tools are matched within TTP's transferred specification (N1, N4)
Execute test vehicle full-flow wafer runs: minimum 5 lots × 25 wafers, from substrate preparation through final metallization and passivation (N1, N2)

Collect PCM/WAT electrical measurement data on all device structures: NMOS/PMOS Vth, Ion, Ioff, Idsat, Rs, Rpoly, Rcont, BV, Cox, SRAM Vccmin — compare to TTP's transferred specification targets (N1, N5)
Identify and resolve process excursions: use split-lot DOE to isolate top-3 yield limiters per module; build defect root-cause library targeting ≥ 80% coverage of observed failure modes (N1, N2)
Calibrate SPICE models from Green Fab test vehicle silicon: fit BSIM4 parameters (Vth0, k1, VSAT, etc.) to WAT data — target Ids error < ±5% across all bias points (N3)
Validate DRC/LVS/PEX rule decks on test chip layouts: run Calibre DRC on reference test chip — target 0 errors; run LVS — target 0 mismatches (N3)
Define 6 process corners (SS/TT/FF/SF/FS at −40°C/27°C/125°C) from Green Fab silicon data; characterize standard cell timing (Liberty .lib) (N2, N3)
Release PDK v0.9 package to first customer design team: SPICE models, DRC/LVS/PEX decks, standard cells, Liberty .lib, PCells (N3)
Complete process FMEA for all critical modules: identify failure modes, effects, severity, occurrence, detection ratings — address all high-RPN items (N6)
Build defect playbook: classify top-10 observed defect modes by root cause, morphology, and spatial signature (N1, N6)
Commission SPC, FDC, and APC systems using test vehicle data: tune thresholds, validate APC model structure, deploy YMS (N7)

Initiate reliability qualification test lots from P2 silicon: load HTOL chambers at 125°C [JESD22-A108F]; start HAST at 130°C/85%RH [JESD22-A110E]; initiate TC cycling at −65°C to +150°C [JESD22-A104E] (N6)
deliverables: Functional yield ≥ 60% on test vehicle wafers — documented across ≥ 3 consecutive full-flow lots with statistical process capability data
PDK v0.9 released to first customer design team — package: SPICE/corner models, DRC/LVS/PEX decks, standard cell library (Liberty .lib, GDSII, CDL), SRAM compiler, ESD clamps, IO cells
First customer test chip taped out and under manufacture on Green Fab's process
Process corners defined: SS/TT/FF/SF/FS at three temperatures — signed off by N2 and N3
SPICE model accuracy report: Ids fit error < ±5% across all device types and bias conditions
Defect playbook v1.0: top-10 defect modes classified with root cause, spatial signature, and corrective action
Process FMEA completed and reviewed: all high-RPN items have mitigation plans implemented (N6)
SPC control charts operational for all critical parameters — limits set from P2 statistical data
FDC rules deployed and tuned on P2 process data — false alarm rate < 5%, fault detection rate > 95% for major fault types
APC R2R models commissioned for litho CD, etch depth, and CMP removal rate
Reliability qualification lots initiated and confirmed loaded in stress chambers (HTOL, HAST, TC)
Reliability test log: sample sizes, stress conditions, vehicle description, interim readout schedule [JEDEC JEP001-3A]
refs: JEDEC JEP013B — Technology Qualification Vehicle Testing (uploaded, image-PDF)
JEDEC JEP012A — Foundry Process Qualification, Front End Transistor Level (uploaded, image-PDF)
JEDEC JEP013A — Foundry Process Qualification, Product Level (uploaded, image-PDF)
JEDEC JEP131A — Potential Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) Publication (uploaded, image-PDF)
May, G. S. & Spanos, C. J., "Fundamentals of Semiconductor Manufacturing and Process Control," Sec. 5.7 (uploaded, text-verified)
entry_criteria: All critical-path tools SAT-accepted (P1b complete); cleanroom certified (P1c complete); all T1 IP deliverables received (P1a complete); process recipes loaded on Green Fab tools; basic PDK framework available.
exit_criteria: Functional/Production yield ≥ 60% (P2) / 80% (P3) — user-specified target. Additional exit criteria: Functional yield ≥ 60% on test vehicle wafer (full flow, multiple lots); PDK v0.9 released to at least one customer design team; process corners (SS/TT/FF) defined and characterized; SPICE models calibrated to Green Fab silicon; defect playbook started (top-10 defect modes classified); FMEA completed for all critical process modules; SPC, FDC, and APC systems operational.

label: Phase 3
title: Qualified Product · PDK v1.0 · HVM ≥ 80%
description: Phase 3 — Qualified Product, PDK v1.0, and Volume Ramp drives the technology from test-vehicle readiness into production. Reliability qualification completes with HTOL 1000 hr biased at junction temperature, HAST 130°C/85%RH/96 hr biased, and TC −65°C to +150°C / 1000 cycles, with sample sizes per JEDEC statistical rules; failure-mechanism activation energies and degradation models follow JEDEC JEP122H Table 6-1 (TDDB gate-ox Eaa=0.7 eV, BEoL/low-k Eaa=0.75 eV, Al EM Eaa=0.8 eV with Black's J² law, NBTI Eaa=0.55 eV time-scaling) [1]. Customer Process Qualification Engineering (PQE) acceptance is obtained per the JEDEC Product-Level Foundry Qualification JEP013A [2]. Yield is driven from ≥60% (P2 exit) to ≥80% (HVM commitment) through systematic split-lot DOEs, Customer-Specific Product Engineering IP accumulates per design tape-in. SPC and APC at full production volume follow May & Spanos Ch. 6 (SPC) and Ch. 9 (Run-by-Run APC) [3]. PDK v1.0 is released to production. Quality Management System reaches full ISO 9001:2015 [4] (or IATF 16949 for automotive fabs) audit certification. Phase exit is the technology-end-of-life decision.
activities: Optimize process recipes specifically for customer product layout: use design-process interaction analysis to identify and resolve customer-specific yield limiters; drive wafer sort yield from P2 baseline (≥60%) to ≥ 80% (N1, N2)
Lock all production module recipes: no experiments on qualification and production lots; implement formal PCN (Process Change Notification) procedure for all future modifications (N1, N2)
Complete HTOL 1000-hour readout: analyze time-to-fail using Weibull/lognormal statistics; calculate FIT rate with 60% confidence interval per JEDEC JESD85 (N6)
Complete HAST 96-hour readout and Temperature Cycling 1000-cycle readout: confirm 0 failures per JEDEC JEP001-3A zero-failure qualification (N6)

Complete PDK v1.0 with production-grade characterization: update SPICE/corner models from P3 silicon (multi-lot, multi-wafer), complete SRAM compiler characterization, finalize DFY guidelines from yield learning data (N3)
Support multiple concurrent customer tape-outs: respond to DRC/LVS signoff queries, model accuracy questions within 24-hour SLA (N3)
Prepare qualification report: HTOL/HAST/TC results, FA of any failures, FIT rate summary, acceleration factor derivation, DPPM estimate (N6)
Conduct customer PQE review meeting: present full qualification data; obtain written PQE acceptance signature
Issue Certificate of Conformance (CoC) for first production lots (N6)
Establish ongoing reliability monitoring program: quarterly HTOL sampling from production lots (N6)
Achieve SEMI E10 equipment availability targets ≥ 85% for all critical-path tools; deploy predictive maintenance models (N4)
Deploy virtual metrology models in closed-loop APC control; optimize metrology sampling plan for HVM (N5, N7)
deliverables: Production yield ≥ 80% at wafer sort — documented across ≥ 5 consecutive lots of customer product with Cpk ≥ 1.33 on critical electrical parameters
All production module recipes locked (version-controlled, revision history, PCN process operational for future changes)

PDK v1.0 released — production-grade package: SPICE/corner models (multi-lot calibrated), complete DRC/LVS/PEX decks, full standard cell library, SRAM compiler, ESD clamps, IO libraries, DFY guidelines
HTOL 1000-hour qualification completed: JEDEC JEP001-3A qualification criteria met; FIT rate calculated per JESD85 with 60% confidence interval
HAST 96-hour qualification completed: 0 failures
Temperature Cycling 1000-cycle qualification completed: 0 failures
Full qualification report: all stress test results, FA summaries, FIT rate, DPPM estimate, acceleration factors
Customer PQE sign-off: written acceptance document from customer's Product Quality Engineer
Certificate of Conformance (CoC) issued for first production lot per ISO 9001:2015
HVM production ramp: committed wafer start rate achieved with SEMI E10 availability ≥ 85% on critical tools
Ongoing reliability monitoring program established: quarterly HTOL sampling schedule and trending methodology documented
refs: JEDEC JEP122H — Failure Mechanisms and Models, Table 6-1 (uploaded, text-verified)
JEDEC JEP013A — Foundry Process Qualification, Product Level (uploaded, image-PDF)
May & Spanos Ch. 6 (SPC) and Ch. 9 (Run-by-Run APC) (uploaded, text-verified)
ISO 9001:2015 / IATF 16949 (training-derived; not uploaded)
entry_criteria: Test vehicle yield ≥ 60% (P2 exit); PDK v0.9 released and customer test chip taped out; reliability qualification lots started; process recipes locked (no experiments).
exit_criteria: Functional/Production yield ≥ 60% (P2) / 80% (P3) — user-specified target. Additional exit criteria: Customer product yield ≥ 80% (wafer sort, multiple consecutive lots); PDK v1.0 released (production-grade); HTOL/HAST/TC reliability qualification passed (JEDEC/AEC-Q100); FIT rate calculated and reported; customer PQE sign-off obtained; Certificate of Conformance (CoC) issued; HVM production ramp established at committed wafer start rate.
image_caption: Luồng quy trình HVM hoàn chỉnh
